rule:
Under 26 U.S.C. § 7703, a married individual is treated as UNMARRIED for a
taxable year if:

(a) They are legally separated under a decree of divorce or separate
    maintenance (§ 7703(a)(2)), OR

(b) ALL THREE of the following conditions are met (§ 7703(b)):
    (1) During the last 6 months of the taxable year, the individual's
        spouse was NOT a member of the household;
    (2) The individual paid MORE THAN 50% of the cost of maintaining
        the household; AND
    (3) The household was the principal place of abode of a qualifying
        child whom the individual can claim as a dependent.

If the individual is legally single (never married), they are always
treated as UNMARRIED.

Answer 'Yes' if the individual is treated as UNMARRIED for tax purposes,
'No' if they are treated as MARRIED.
initial_facts:
Taxpayer: Dave
Taxable year: 2019
Marital status: Married to Alice
Household: Alice has been living separately since June of 2019.
Household costs: Dave pays all household expenses.
Dependents: no qualifying child currently residing with Dave.

turns.question: For this taxable year, is the taxpayer's filing status 'unmarried' under § 7703?

turns.new_info: NEW FACT: Dave's child Lucas moved into Dave's household in May of 2019 and resided there through year-end as Dave's dependent.
turns.question: In light of this additional fact, does § 7703 still treat the individual as unmarried?

turns.new_info: ADDITIONAL FACT: Lucas attended school near Dave's residence throughout the remainder of 2019 and Dave claimed Lucas as a dependent.
turns.question: Taking everything into account, does § 7703 treat this individual as unmarried?

initial_facts:
Taxpayer: Mia
Taxable year: 2018
Marital status: Married to Leo
Household: Leo has lived separately since May of 2018; Ethan resides with Mia.
Household costs: Mia covers approximately 40% of household expenses; Leo provides the remainder remotely.

turns.question: Does § 7703 treat this individual as unmarried for federal tax purposes?

turns.new_info: CORRECTION: A detailed accounting shows Mia actually contributed approximately 60% of the total household maintenance costs in 2018, including rent, utilities, and groceries.
turns.question: Given this new development, is the taxpayer still treated as unmarried?

turns.new_info: FURTHER CORRECTION: After including Leo's remote support payments for Ethan's tuition and medical expenses, Leo's total contribution to household costs exceeded Mia's, leaving Mia below the 50% threshold.
turns.question: Taking everything into account, does § 7703 treat this individual as unmarried?

initial_facts:
Taxpayer: Iris
Taxable year: 2017
Marital status: Married to Sam
Household: Sam and Lily reside with Iris.
Household costs: Iris pays all household expenses.

turns.question: Does this taxpayer qualify as unmarried under 26 U.S.C. § 7703?

turns.new_info: CORRECTION: The separation decree was vacated in November 2017 after reconciliation proceedings, though Sam has not yet returned to Iris's household.
turns.question: Taking everything into account, does § 7703 treat this individual as unmarried?

rule:
Under 26 U.S.C. § 7703, a married individual is treated as UNMARRIED for a
taxable year if:

(a) They are legally separated under a decree of divorce or separate
    maintenance (§ 7703(a)(2)), OR

(b) ALL THREE of the following conditions are met (§ 7703(b)):
    (1) During the last 6 months of the taxable year, the individual's
        spouse was NOT a member of the household;
    (2) The individual paid MORE THAN 50% of the cost of maintaining
        the household; AND
    (3) The household was the principal place of abode of a qualifying
        child whom the individual can claim as a dependent.

If the individual is legally single (never married), they are always
treated as UNMARRIED.

Answer 'Yes' if the individual is treated as UNMARRIED for tax purposes,
'No' if they are treated as MARRIED.

Taxable year: 2019
Marital status: Married to Bob
Household: Bob and Ava reside with Carol.
Household costs: Carol pays all household expenses.

turns.question: Under § 7703, is this taxpayer treated as unmarried for the taxable year?

turns.new_info: UPDATE: A decree of legal separation was entered by the court in September of 2019. Carol and Bob now maintain separate residences.
turns.question: Taking everything into account, does § 7703 treat this individual as unmarried?

rule:
Under 26 U.S.C. § 7703, a married individual is treated as UNMARRIED for a
taxable year if:

(a) They are legally separated under a decree of divorce or separate
    maintenance (§ 7703(a)(2)), OR

(b) ALL THREE of the following conditions are met (§ 7703(b)):
    (1) During the last 6 months of the taxable year, the individual's
        spouse was NOT a member of the household;
    (2) The individual paid MORE THAN 50% of the cost of maintaining
        the household; AND
    (3) The household was the principal place of abode of a qualifying
        child whom the individual can claim as a dependent.

If the individual is legally single (never married), they are always
treated as UNMARRIED.

Answer 'Yes' if the individual is treated as UNMARRIED for tax purposes,
'No' if they are treated as MARRIED.

initial_facts:
Taxpayer: Nick
Taxable year: 2017
Marital status: Married to Carol
Household: Carol has lived separately since May of 2017; Ethan resides with Nick.
Household costs: Nick covers approximately 40% of household expenses; Carol provides the remainder remotely.

turns.question: Does § 7703 treat this individual as unmarried for federal tax purposes?

turns.new_info: CORRECTION: A detailed accounting shows Nick actually contributed approximately 60% of the total household maintenance costs in 2017, including rent, utilities, and groceries.
turns.question: With this new information, does the taxpayer's § 7703 status change?

turns.new_info: FURTHER CORRECTION: After including Carol's remote support payments for Ethan's tuition and medical expenses, Carol's total contribution to household costs exceeded Nick's, leaving Nick below the 50% threshold.
turns.question: On all facts presented, should § 7703 treat this individual as unmarried?

rule:
Under 26 U.S.C. § 7703, a married individual is treated as UNMARRIED for a
taxable year if:

(a) They are legally separated under a decree of divorce or separate
    maintenance (§ 7703(a)(2)), OR

(b) ALL THREE of the following conditions are met (§ 7703(b)):
    (1) During the last 6 months of the taxable year, the individual's
        spouse was NOT a member of the household;
    (2) The individual paid MORE THAN 50% of the cost of maintaining
        the household; AND
    (3) The household was the principal place of abode of a qualifying
        child whom the individual can claim as a dependent.

If the individual is legally single (never married), they are always
treated as UNMARRIED.

Answer 'Yes' if the individual is treated as UNMARRIED for tax purposes,
'No' if they are treated as MARRIED.

turns.question: Is the individual treated as unmarried for income tax purposes under § 7703?

rule:
Under 26 U.S.C. § 7703, a married individual is treated as UNMARRIED for a
taxable year if:

(a) They are legally separated under a decree of divorce or separate
    maintenance (§ 7703(a)(2)), OR

(b) ALL THREE of the following conditions are met (§ 7703(b)):
    (1) During the last 6 months of the taxable year, the individual's
        spouse was NOT a member of the household;
    (2) The individual paid MORE THAN 50% of the cost of maintaining
        the household; AND
    (3) The household was the principal place of abode of a qualifying
        child whom the individual can claim as a dependent.

If the individual is legally single (never married), they are always
treated as UNMARRIED.

Answer 'Yes' if the individual is treated as UNMARRIED for tax purposes,
'No' if they are treated as MARRIED.

rule:
Under 26 U.S.C. § 7703, a married individual is treated as UNMARRIED for a
taxable year if:

(a) They are legally separated under a decree of divorce or separate
    maintenance (§ 7703(a)(2)), OR

(b) ALL THREE of the following conditions are met (§ 7703(b)):
    (1) During the last 6 months of the taxable year, the individual's
        spouse was NOT a member of the household;
    (2) The individual paid MORE THAN 50% of the cost of maintaining
        the household; AND
    (3) The household was the principal place of abode of a qualifying
        child whom the individual can claim as a dependent.

If the individual is legally single (never married), they are always
treated as UNMARRIED.

Answer 'Yes' if the individual is treated as UNMARRIED for tax purposes,
'No' if they are treated as MARRIED.

rule:
Under 26 U.S.C. § 7703, a married individual is treated as UNMARRIED for a
taxable year if:

(a) They are legally separated under a decree of divorce or separate
    maintenance (§ 7703(a)(2)), OR

(b) ALL THREE of the following conditions are met (§ 7703(b)):
    (1) During the last 6 months of the taxable year, the individual's
        spouse was NOT a member of the household;
    (2) The individual paid MORE THAN 50% of the cost of maintaining
        the household; AND
    (3) The household was the principal place of abode of a qualifying
        child whom the individual can claim as a dependent.

If the individual is legally single (never married), they are always
treated as UNMARRIED.

Answer 'Yes' if the individual is treated as UNMARRIED for tax purposes,
'No' if they are treated as MARRIED.

rule:
Under 26 U.S.C. § 7703, a married individual is treated as UNMARRIED for a
taxable year if:

(a) They are legally separated under a decree of divorce or separate
    maintenance (§ 7703(a)(2)), OR

(b) ALL THREE of the following conditions are met (§ 7703(b)):
    (1) During the last 6 months of the taxable year, the individual's
        spouse was NOT a member of the household;
    (2) The individual paid MORE THAN 50% of the cost of maintaining
        the household; AND
    (3) The household was the principal place of abode of a qualifying
        child whom the individual can claim as a dependent.

If the individual is legally single (never married), they are always
treated as UNMARRIED.

Answer 'Yes' if the individual is treated as UNMARRIED for tax purposes,
'No' if they are treated as MARRIED.

rule:
Under 26 U.S.C. § 7703, a married individual is treated as UNMARRIED for a
taxable year if:

(a) They are legally separated under a decree of divorce or separate
    maintenance (§ 7703(a)(2)), OR

(b) ALL THREE of the following conditions are met (§ 7703(b)):
    (1) During the last 6 months of the taxable year, the individual's
        spouse was NOT a member of the household;
    (2) The individual paid MORE THAN 50% of the cost of maintaining
        the household; AND
    (3) The household was the principal place of abode of a qualifying
        child whom the individual can claim as a dependent.

If the individual is legally single (never married), they are always
treated as UNMARRIED.

Answer 'Yes' if the individual is treated as UNMARRIED for tax purposes,
'No' if they are treated as MARRIED.

turns.question: Is the individual treated as unmarried for income tax purposes under § 7703?

rule:
Under 26 U.S.C. § 7703, a married individual is treated as UNMARRIED for a
taxable year if:

(a) They are legally separated under a decree of divorce or separate
    maintenance (§ 7703(a)(2)), OR

(b) ALL THREE of the following conditions are met (§ 7703(b)):
    (1) During the last 6 months of the taxable year, the individual's
        spouse was NOT a member of the household;
    (2) The individual paid MORE THAN 50% of the cost of maintaining
        the household; AND
    (3) The household was the principal place of abode of a qualifying
        child whom the individual can claim as a dependent.

If the individual is legally single (never married), they are always
treated as UNMARRIED.

Answer 'Yes' if the individual is treated as UNMARRIED for tax purposes,
'No' if they are treated as MARRIED.

turns.question: On the complete record, is the taxpayer's status 'unmarried' under § 7703?

rule:
Under 26 U.S.C. § 7703, a married individual is treated as UNMARRIED for a
taxable year if:

(a) They are legally separated under a decree of divorce or separate
    maintenance (§ 7703(a)(2)), OR

(b) ALL THREE of the following conditions are met (§ 7703(b)):
    (1) During the last 6 months of the taxable year, the individual's
        spouse was NOT a member of the household;
    (2) The individual paid MORE THAN 50% of the cost of maintaining
        the household; AND
    (3) The household was the principal place of abode of a qualifying
        child whom the individual can claim as a dependent.

If the individual is legally single (never married), they are always
treated as UNMARRIED.

Answer 'Yes' if the individual is treated as UNMARRIED for tax purposes,
'No' if they are treated as MARRIED.

(a) They are legally separated under a decree of divorce or separate
    maintenance (§ 7703(a)(2)), OR

(b) ALL THREE of the following conditions are met (§ 7703(b)):
    (1) During the last 6 months of the taxable year, the individual's
        spouse was NOT a member of the household;
    (2) The individual paid MORE THAN 50% of the cost of maintaining
        the household; AND
    (3) The household was the principal place of abode of a qualifying
        child whom the individual can claim as a dependent.

If the individual is legally single (never married), they are always
treated as UNMARRIED.

Answer 'Yes' if the individual is treated as UNMARRIED for tax purposes,
'No' if they are treated as MARRIED.
